label: Recent CS grad targeting AI APM at Anthropic
resume_text:
Eli Chen
elichen@stanford.edu | linkedin.com/in/elichen | github.com/elichen

EDUCATION
Stanford University, B.S. Computer Science (2021-2025)
GPA 3.85/4.0 | Minor in Symbolic Systems | Phi Beta Kappa
Relevant coursework: CS 224N (NLP), CS 229 (ML), CS 246 (Mining Massive Datasets), CS 142 (Web Apps)
Senior Thesis: Calibration Metrics for LLM-Generated Code Reviews (advisor: Prof. Christopher Potts)

EXPERIENCE
SWE Intern, Anthropic (Summer 2024)
- Built internal eval dashboard for Claude code-completion quality across 12 programming languages
- Designed regression test suite that caught 3 quality regressions during the Sonnet 4.4 to 4.5 rollout
- Shipped MCP server for company knowledge base used by 40+ engineers
- Tools: Python, FastAPI, Postgres, Model Context Protocol

ML Intern, Scale AI (Summer 2023)
- Improved labeler agreement metrics (Krippendorff's alpha) for autonomous-driving annotation pipeline
- Built active-learning pipeline that reduced labeling cost by 23% on internal benchmark
- Tools: Python, PyTorch, Snowflake, internal labeling tooling

Research Assistant, Stanford NLP Group (2023-2024)
- Co-authored Confidence Calibration in Code Generation, published EMNLP 2024 (third author)
- Implemented faithfulness eval suite on HumanEval+ that became part of group's standard tooling

TA, CS 106B (Programming Abstractions), Stanford (Winter 2024)
- Held weekly office hours for 30+ students; received 4.8/5.0 student rating

PROJECTS
ai-pr-reviewer (1.2k stars on GitHub) - Open-source GitHub Action that flags PR test coverage gaps using Claude. Adopted by 8 small open-source projects.
bookface-but-better - Personal Bookface clone using LangGraph for thread summarization. ~30 active users from Stanford CS network.

SKILLS
Python (5 yrs), TypeScript (3 yrs), PyTorch, JAX, LangGraph, FastAPI, Postgres, Docker, Model Context Protocol, prompt engineering, eval design
